governingInstruments.name: Proceeds of Crime Act 2002
platformImplications: SAR existence must never be disclosed on platform
Signals must not imply ongoing investigation
Tipping-off prohibition: use neutral language only

governingInstruments.name: Terrorism Act 2000
platformImplications: Parallel obligations for TF signals
Same tipping-off restrictions apply

governingInstruments.name: Money Laundering, Terrorist Financing and Transfer of Funds (Information on the Payer) Regulations 2017
platformImplications: CDD must be performed before data is published to platform
High-risk third country flag must be propagated in signal metadata
5-year data retention minimum (Reg. 40)

governingInstruments.name: Economic Crime and Corporate Transparency Act 2023, ss.188-189
platformImplications: Sharing via platform qualifies as indirect sharing under warning condition only
Platform is third-party intermediary: warning condition must be met before upload
Protections are domestic-only: cross-border relay requires separate legal basis
No request condition permitted for platform-mediated indirect sharing
Platform must maintain transparent governance and security protocols
GDPR compliance is not disapplied by ECCTA

warningCondition.description: Sending firm must have taken or would have taken safeguarding action against the customer

governingInstruments.name: Financial Services and Markets Act 2023, s.72
platformImplications: APP fraud reimbursement is mandatory for FPS/CHAPS payments
Signal type APP_FRAUD must be flagged separately from UNAUTHORISED_TRANSACTION
50:50 cost-share between sending and receiving PSP informs signal routing

governingInstruments.name: PSR Specific Direction 20 (FPS APP Scams Reimbursement Requirement)
platformImplications: APP fraud signals must include CDRS-compatible fields for Pay.UK reporting
Signal must record: payment amount, fraud type category, consumer claim status, exception applied
Data retained in RCMS-compatible format from 1 May 2025

governingInstruments.name: PSR Specific Direction 21 (CHAPS APP Scams Reimbursement Requirement)
platformImplications: Applies same rules as SD20 for CHAPS payments
Signal must flag payment rail: FPS or CHAPS

governingInstruments.name: Payment Services Regulations 2017, Reg. 76
platformImplications: Unauthorised payment signals: PSP liability triggers end of next business day
Signal must include T+0 timestamp of PSP awareness of unauthorised transaction

governingInstruments.name: UK GDPR and Data Protection Act 2018
platformImplications: Data minimisation: only fields necessary for fraud typology identification
Purpose limitation: signals may only be used for AML/fraud prevention
Retention: minimum 5 years, maximum as per AML retention schedule
DPIA required before platform deployment (high-risk processing)
No direct identifiers in shared signal payload

governingInstruments.name: Data (Use and Access) Act 2025
platformImplications: Cross-border transfers subject to materially lower standard than EU essential equivalence
Adequacy decisions issued by Secretary of State
IDTA or UK Addendum to EU SCCs required for non-adequacy jurisdictions

governingInstruments.name: ICO Anonymisation and Pseudonymisation Guidance (March 2025)
platformImplications: Motivated-intruder test applies to all shared fields
Unsalted hashing is insufficient pseudonymisation
MD5 and SHA-1 are deprecated for pseudonymisation
HMAC-SHA256 with per-partnership salt is minimum standard
FPE permitted for format-preserving identifier transformation

appFraudReimbursementCap.legalBasis: FSMA 2023 s.72 / PSR SD20 / PSR SD21
